attacking_style: Pure sprinter who finishes field sprints rather than initiating attacks. Most effective in final 200m with raw speed, less reliant on perfect positioning than positioning-dependent sprinters.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal lead-out support, often finishing without organized team train. Relies on individual positioning and raw speed rather than coordinated setup.
terrain_preference: Flat field sprints and uphill finishes where raw power trumps positioning. Shows surprising competence on cobbles (E3 fifth place) and can handle technical finales. Struggles with late-race bridging efforts on descents.
vulnerability: Lacks team support for consistent lead-out positioning. Cannot bridge small gaps (10 seconds) in final 2km despite technical skills. Inconsistent results when perfect positioning required (sixth at Bretagne despite good position).
key_patterns: Wins head-to-head sprints with minimal lead-out, beating same rivals repeatedly (2 observations)
Performs well on uphill finishes without team support, neo-pro finishing second chaotically
Shows cobbled classics potential, fifth at E3 age 21 debut performance
Cannot close small gaps in final 2km, failed 10-second bridge despite descent skills
Form disrupted by February 2023 crash, returned to winning by June Tour de Suisse
form_trajectory: positive: Returned to winning form at Tour de Suisse June 2023 after February crash recovery, timing peak for Tour debut perfectly
